title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: WATCH LIVE: UFC fighters hold press conference from Lincoln Memorial
stories.summary:
WATCH LIVE: UFC fighters hold press conference from Lincoln Memorial. UFC fighters are holding a news conference at the Lincoln Memorial ahead of their fights on the White House lawn as part of the UFC Freedom 250 fight.

The press conference is scheduled to begin at 8:15 p.m.

Eastern time. UFC FREEDOM 250 FULL STEAM AHEAD: OBAMA JUDGE DECLINES TO STOP SUNDAY FIGHT UFC CEO […].

Al Jazeera: US judge refuses to block Trump’s White House UFC fight. Al Jazeera reports that federal judge rules UFC fight at White House on Trump’s birthday can proceed despite legal challenges.

stories.title: Report says UAE released billions of dollars to Iran but foreign ministry denies transfer
stories.summary:
Report says UAE released billions of dollars to Iran but foreign ministry denies transfer. The United Arab Emirates released billions of dollars in frozen Iranian assets as an olive branch to ward off further attacks, according to a report.

Four sources told Reuters that the UAE agreed to release $10 to $20 billion in frozen Iranian assets, $3 billion of which has been released, in exchange for Iran halting […]. Al Jazeera: UAE to unlock frozen Iranian funds amid US ceasefire push: Sources.

Al Jazeera reports that reuters reports UAE agreed to unlock billions for Iran, but Abu Dhabi swiftly issued a categorical denial.

stories.title: Judge rejects bid to keep Trump's name on Kennedy Center
stories.summary:
Judge rejects bid to keep Trump's name on Kennedy Center. A federal judge denied a last-ditch effort to stop the removal of President Trump's name from the Kennedy Center while the appeals process plays out.

Al Jazeera: Judge keeps order in place to remove Trump’s name from Kennedy Center.

Al Jazeera reports that the US president has sought to reshape the capital city's image and institutions through series of plans and projects.

stories.title: US killed Tren de Aragua leader in coordination with Venezuela, Trump says
stories.summary:
US killed Tren de Aragua leader in coordination with Venezuela, Trump says. President Donald Trump announced that the head of the Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua, Héctor Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, also known as Nino Guerrero, was killed in a U.S. airstrike, in coordination with the Venezuelan government.

“At my direction, the United States Southern Command delivered a swift and lethal kinetic strike to successfully execute Niño Guerrero, […]. Fox News: Trump says US military eliminated 'infamous' Tren de Aragua leader in lethal strike.

Fox News reports that president Donald Trump announced a U.S. military strike that killed Tren de Aragua leader Niño Guerrero, calling it retribution for victims of violent crime.

stories.title: Kids Can’t Even Run A Lemonade Stand In Dem-Run City
stories.summary:
Kids Can’t Even Run A Lemonade Stand In Dem-Run City. An 11-year-old girl and her 12-year-old brother operating a lemonade stand in Boston got a brutal lesson in urban lawlessness Wednesday afternoon when a pair of juvenile suspects robbed them at gunpoint.

It was roughly 4:44 p.m. when the suspects first appeared, casing the stand with multiple walk-bys before finally approaching the children and asking ... Breitbart: VIDEO — Police: Children's Lemonade Stand Robbed at Gunpoint in Democrat-Controlled Boston.

Breitbart reports that two children were robbed while running a lemonade stand on Wednesday afternoon in a Boston neighborhood, and the incident has shaken community members. The post VIDEO — Police: Children’s Lemonade Stand Robbed at Gunpoint in Democrat-Controlled Boston appeared first on Breitbart.

stories.title: At Least Two Dead, Multiple Injured In Texas Shooting
stories.summary:
At Least Two Dead, Multiple Injured In Texas Shooting. Two people were killed, and 10 victims were reported after a shooting in Midland, Texas, on Friday morning.

Authorities confirmed the suspect is dead after being barricaded inside a building.

Officers responding to the scene encountered gunfire, according to The Texas Tribune. BREAKING: Police are responding to an “active shooter event” in Midland, Texas pic.twitter.com/Kd7UXEOq68 ...

Breitbart: Midland, Texas, Shooting Suspect Was Wanted for Attempted Murder of Police Officer. Breitbart reports that the man believed to have shot and wounded ten and killed one Friday morning in Midland, Texas, was wanted for the attempted murder of a police officer.

The post Midland, Texas, Shooting Suspect Was Wanted for Attempted Murder of Police Officer appeared first on Breitbart .<.

stories.title: Trucking in a fuel crisis: the Australian drivers sacrificing their paycheck for diesel – video
stories.summary:
Trucking in a fuel crisis: the Australian drivers sacrificing their paycheck for diesel – video. Frank is a truck owner-driver, meaning he owns his own truck, picks up contracts wherever he happens to be in Australia and pays for his own fuel.

He can spend up to two months at a time on the road, and lives by the rule-of-thumb that one third of a job's pay should go to fuel, one third should go to truck maintenance and the remaining third is his wages. But the war in Iran, leading to increased diesel prices, has put significant financial strain on this model.

Coupled with historically sli.

stories.title: AI ‘art’ is boring, soulless theft – and when I see it as an artist I see red | Jess Harwood
stories.summary:
AI ‘art’ is boring, soulless theft – and when I see it as an artist I see red | Jess Harwood. I draw the old way – with my hand.

Doing it with AI would not make me more creative, it would drain the colour out of my existence Last week I went to a gig by myself for the first time.

I sat myself down in my single seat, possibly the youngest person in the room and one of thousands excited to see Split Enz. I loved it – I felt joy and heartache as the lyrics spoke of human experiences, really lived.

I happily realised that I did not have to wonder whether Split Enz had used AI in the.

stories.title: Derbyshire police officer investigated over AI-generated ‘evidential material’
stories.summary:
Derbyshire police officer investigated over AI-generated ‘evidential material’.

Unidentified officer removed from frontline duties in the first known case of its kind in the UK A police officer is under criminal investigation over the alleged use of artificial intelligence and has been removed from frontline duties in the first known case of its kind in the UK.

The officer, who has not been named, is being investigated over allegations of using the technology to “create evidential material in a number of cases” and perverting the course of justice. <a hre.

stories.title: The Guardian view on the analogue resurgence: the shock of the old | Editorial
stories.summary:
The Guardian view on the analogue resurgence: the shock of the old | Editorial.

Long-abandoned formats such as cassettes and VHS tapes are finding new life as consumers seek a digital detox Ten years after the last video recorder manufacturer ceased production, the first straight-to-video movie for two decades – This Is How the World Ends – was released this month.

The resurgence of vinyl began long ago; sales are at their highest l.

stories.summary: How big tech got its way on Trump’s AI executive order. stories.title: Facebook users report outages, log-in issues
stories.summary:
Facebook users report outages, log-in issues. MANILA, Philippines — Thousands of Facebook users reported problems accessing their accounts Friday night.

Facebook appeared to have experienced a major global outage, as many users cited log-in issues, disruptions affecting other Meta-owned platforms, including Messenger and Instagram.

READ: PNP backs DICT, PCO call for stricter Meta action vs fake news, disinformation Users reported being. Rappler: Thousands of users report Facebook, Messenger outage.

Rappler reports that facebook and Messenger experience widespread outage, affecting users globally as reports surge on social media platforms.

stories.title: DENR probes contractors in earth-balling of trees on Roxas Blvd.
stories.summary:
DENR probes contractors in earth-balling of trees on Roxas Blvd..

MANILA, Philippines — The Department of Environment Natural Resources is already investigating the contractors involved in the reported earth-balling activities along Roxas Boulevard, with a specific focus on whether their methods violated established environmental protocols and the conditions of their permits.

DENR Enforcement personnel have been deployed to conduct an immediate site inspection and verify.

stories.title: New guide helps islands cut waste, build resilience
stories.summary:
New guide helps islands cut waste, build resilience. MANILA, Philippines — A new regional framework is urging island communities to move beyond traditional waste management and adopt a broader approach that links waste reduction with food security, water conservation, energy resilience and local livelihoods.

Released by the Global Alliance for Incinerator Alternatives Asia Pacific, “Pathways Towards Circularity: The Zero Waste Islands Framework” presents.

stories.title: INQUIRER SPECIAL: Agusan Del Sur’s Naliyagan fest: National pride, local identity
stories.summary:
INQUIRER SPECIAL: Agusan Del Sur’s Naliyagan fest: National pride, local identity.

PROSPERIDAD, AGUSAN DEL SUR—As the nation commemorates Philippine Independence Day today, Agusan del Sur is marking the occasion by opening the Naliyagan Festival 2026, blending patriotism, cultural heritage, and community celebration into one of the province’s biggest annual events.

The opening program is expected to combine national pride with local identity, transforming Independence Day into.

stories.title: Old freedom, new struggles take center stage in Independence day rites
stories.summary:
Old freedom, new struggles take center stage in Independence day rites. BAGUIO CITY—Filipinos across the country commemorated the 128th anniversary of Philippine Independence on Friday, honoring the sacrifices of the nation’s heroes while highlighting the continuing struggle for peace, economic resilience, energy security and social justice.

In Baguio City, families visited the museum of Gen. Emilio Aguinaldo to view what his descendants believe is the original.

stories.title: U.N. and Congolese Troops Struggle to Halt Lightning Rebel Attack
stories.summary: U.N. and Congolese Troops Struggle to Halt Lightning Rebel Attack. Aid workers estimate as many as 300,000 people are fleeing advances by the Rwandan-backed M23 rebel group, which has taken two strategic towns on the approaches to Goma over the past several days.

stories.title: Meta’s Edits app is getting an AI assistant and a desktop version
stories.summary: Meta’s Edits app is getting an AI assistant and a desktop version. By integrating an AI assistant directly into Edits, Meta is aiming to keep creators engaged on Instagram as it continues to compete with TikTok and YouTube for creators' attention.

stories.title: Trump Says He Wants to 'Clean Out' Gaza, Send Refugees to Egypt and Jordan
stories.summary: Trump Says He Wants to 'Clean Out' Gaza, Send Refugees to Egypt and Jordan. The president, calling the heavily bombed enclave “a demolition site,” proposed relocating Palestinians temporarily or long-term, a move rejected by Arab countries since the war began.

stories.title: Kim Jong Un Is Doing Everything He Can to Keep North Korea's Youth in Line
stories.summary: Kim Jong Un Is Doing Everything He Can to Keep North Korea's Youth in Line. The dictator, eager to shut out the influence of Hollywood and K-pop, has been exalting a “shock brigade” of 300,000 teens and 20-somethings drafted into flood reconstruction.
